operating_state: Revenue reached $13.51 billion in Q3, up 101% year-over-year and 88% sequentially, driven by Data Center revenue surging 171% annually from unprecedented AI training and inference demand with visibility extending into next year. Gaming revenue increased 22% annually following channel inventory normalization and GeForce RTX 40 Series demand, while Professional Visualization declined and Automotive grew modestly amid ongoing market adjustments.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded dramatically to 70.1% from 43.5% in the prior year, driven by higher-margin Data Center compute GPU sales mix and reduced inventory provisions. Operating expenses increased 10% annually from higher research and development investments in AI platforms, compensation, and expanded headcount to support growth initiatives.
material_events: Board appointed Melissa Lora as new director and approved an additional $25 billion share repurchase authorization bringing total available to $27.95 billion. Company returned $3.28 billion through share repurchases and paid $99 million in dividends during the quarter. Management announced GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip availability and L40S GPU launches while transitioning certain operations out of China following export control requirements.
macro_exposures: U.S. government expanded export license requirements during Q2 for A100 and H100 products to additional customers and Middle East regions beyond existing China restrictions, with potential expansion to gaming products and networking equipment. Supply chain remains concentrated in Asia-Pacific including Taiwan-based partners, creating vulnerability to geopolitical tensions and export control changes.
forward_outlook: Management expects supply to increase each quarter through next year to meet sustained demand visibility, while launching Grace CPU and Grace Hopper Superchips in Q3 fiscal 2024. Company expanding NVIDIA DGX cloud services and AI Foundations offerings through cloud service provider partnerships with multi-year agreements. Cash flow from operations will decline in Q3 due to approximately $3.81 billion in deferred federal income tax payments.
uncertainty_profile: Product architecture transitions across segments create revenue volatility as customers anticipate new products and channel partners reduce prior-generation inventory. Export control landscape remains fluid with compliance burden potentially driving customer design-out of US semiconductors, while new AI use cases carry demand estimation risks given unproven market trajectories.
